title: MRI-Lab Graz Study Application
description:
Application to conduct a study at the MRI-Lab Graz ( Kopernikusgasse 24, 8010 Graz, Austria). 
University Graz

pages.name: Welcome Page
pages.title: Information about the application
pages.description: Before you enter the survey, be sure to have the following information available

elements.name: general_info
elements.title: Needed information
elements.html:
For a study application at the MRI-Lab Graz we need the following information, which will be asked in detail in this online survey:
  • Contact information about the principal investigator and the executive staff
  • Information about ethical approval
  • Funding information
  • Detailed study information (start/end time, participants)

elements.name: MR_data_info
elements.html:
Some words about your MR-data:
The MRI-Lab fully (and only) supports the Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS) format. That offers the great advantage of easily sharing your data (e.g. OpenNeuro) and using all available BIDS-Apps.
That also means that you will not receive any DICOM-data. Further, structural image data will undergo "defacing" (removal of facial structures from MRI images) and participant meta-data is reduced to BIDS standard. This is necessary to be in line with the General Data Protection Regulation.

elements.name: service
elements.html:
Our service:
  • Complete acquisition for your study is taken care of. You don't (and are not allowed) to use the Scanner.
  • Data management, including BIDS-conversion and optional preprocessing (fmriprep) is taken care of.
  • Sequencens and MR-protocols will be provided unless you have specific ideas, wishes or knowledge.

elements.name: responsibilities
elements.html:
Your responsibilities:
  • Participant managment
  • Preparation of fmri-paradigma (if applicable). We strongly advise using psychopy with our BIDS-extension.
  • Logfile-management (if applicable): Additional storage of logfiles, quality assessment (is everything in the file )

elements.name: agree2start
elements.title: Start application
elements.description: I am sure that I have all the required inforamtion

choices.text: I am sure that I have all the required information for my study and understand the data-handling procedure.

pages.name: Personell
pages.title: Personell
pages.description: Information about principal investigator and contact person.

elements.name: project_type
elements.title: Is this an internal or external project?
elements.description: Internal: you are affiliated with the University of Graz. External: you are not a University of Graz member.

elements.name: arbeitsbereich
elements.title: Arbeitsbereich (work area)
elements.description: Your work area within the Institute of Psychology, University of Graz. See https://psychologie.uni-graz.at/de/ueber-das-institut/arbeitsbereiche/

elements.name: organization
elements.title: Institution / Organization
elements.description: Name of the institution or organization you are affiliated with.

elements.name: external_application_info
elements.html: If you are not a member of the University of Graz, you must pay for each scanner hour. You can complete this survey, but we will contact you for further information about scanner costs.

items.name: PI_email
items.title: Email
items.requiredErrorText: You have to provide the email address of the principal investigator!

elements.name: additional_authors
elements.title: Additional authors / collaborators
elements.description: Optional: add any co-authors or collaborators who should be listed as dataset authors besides the PI.

elements.name: Primary Contact
elements.title: Choose one person to be the primary contact for this project.
elements.description: This person will be called about any subject running issues that need immediate attention (e.g. last-minute cancellation due to problems with scanner/equipment).

pages.name: Ethics_info
pages.title: Ethics
pages.description: If you don't have the approval at the moment, you can finish this application BUT a valid authorisation is mandatory for the study-start-

elements.name: Ethics
elements.title: Do you have valid approval from the ethical board of the University of Graz?

elements.name: ethics_committee
elements.title: Name of the ethics committee
elements.description: The committee that issued the approval.

elements.name: code_ethic
elements.title: Please provide the code from the ethical board

elements.name: date_ethic
elements.title: Please specifiy the date of ethical approval.

elements.name: study_description
elements.title: Short description of your study
elements.description: REQUIRED for bids - dataset description. Please provide at least a few sentences (minimum 50 characters) describing the aim/background of the study.

elements.name: keywords
elements.title: Keywords
elements.description: REQUIRED for bids - dataset description. Add at least 3 keywords describing your study (press enter after each one).

elements.name: timespan
elements.title: Anticipated timespan
elements.description: Proposed start of the study (not pilot measurements or any preparations) and end.

choices.text: Cross sectional (only one timepoints for each participant)

elements.name: funding
elements.title: Do you have funding for scanner use (typically 300â¬ per hour)?

elements.name: funding_details
elements.title: Please provide information about your funding

elements.name: mriqc
elements.html:
Your data will be converted into the BIDS-format and automatically uploaded to a predefined location.

Quality control is realized with MRIQC. This enables state-of-the-art quality indices for structural and functional imaging data.

elements.name: fmriprep
elements.title: Should we run fMRIprep for you?
elements.description: If your study includes functional data, we offer to run fmriprep on your dataset.

elements.name: events_file
elements.title: Provide BIDS-events file
elements.description: YOU must provide a bids-valid events file if you want your data to be preprocessed! We strongly advise you to use psychopy and the in-house developed BIDS-extension. Not applicable if you have resting-state data!
elements.commentText: Not applicable if you have resting-state data!

choices.text: I understand that I have to provide a BIDS-valid events-file.

elements.name: agreement
elements.title: I understand and agree to the following next steps:

choices.text: We will arrange one or more pilot measurement(s) to try and define different sequences and or paradigms.
The PI has to analyse and check the data from the pilot measurement(s)
After the PI has approved the data from the pilot session(s), we will book all your MR-slots (available at the webcalender). You have to double-check every single MR-slot and give your OK.
